title: Why shouldnt I like ADV and the English product
display_title: Why shouldnt I like ADV and the English product
wikitext:
[]: Why "shouldn't" I like ADV and the English product             Quote Post by V » Mon Jun 25, 2007 10:11 pm Apart from just "preferring" the original Japanese, etc.....explain to me, as a fan who only saw the series on Adult Swim first, and never had the DVD releases (in their myriad forms)....... Why should I "not like" ADV, the English-production cast and crew, Tiffany Grant, Amanda Winn-Lee, etc. etc. I mean I hear of a "Commentary of Doom" and such.....but should I downright HATE these people for not providing a massively-indepth analysis of the series, or whatever? Keep in mind, that for Lord of the Rings, we had many "spirited points of disagreement" with Peter Jackson & Co (i.e. XenArwen: why change an established female character into a warrior princess....when the story ALREADY has a "warrior princess", one of the most famous in fantasy novels? (Eowyn); answer; Eowyn doesn't show up until Part II).....but other than that, we were still immensely supportive. So should I be angry at these guys, or like them, or what? Please explain.

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Mon Jun 25, 2007 10:41 pm I think the bigger question is why do you want us to tell you if you should hate ADV or not? You should be able to form your own opinion on this, given the information available. That said, in my opinion, it's OK. Greenfield makes many good points, also discussing the background of the VA's in the first commentary which I thought was interesting. Grant tends to ramble in the commentaries, while Greenfield tries to get back on track. As for McCoy, many people think he's a little nutty, but I personally think he makes a few good points, especially the Kabbalastic symbolism.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Mon Jun 25, 2007 10:53 pm I think the bigger question is why do you want us to tell you if you should hate ADV or not? You should be able to form your own opinion on this, given the information available.

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Mon Jun 25, 2007 10:56 pm V wrote: I don't want to spend literally 6 hours hunting through 5 years worth of old threads.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon Jun 25, 2007 11:02 pm V wrote: I think the bigger question is why do you want us to tell you if you should hate ADV or not? You should be able to form your own opinion on this, given the information available.

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Tue Jun 26, 2007 1:02 am never actually seen the english one, just watched the Japanese with subtitles....saw a few clips of the english one. they totally destroyed fuyutsuki's voice. he was all...mr. cool professor and what not. gendo is gendo. just sounds more american. like him better like with a Japanese monotone. anyways thats my 2 centz. i haven't officially heard anything from english dub. so call me crazy.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Jun 26, 2007 1:23 am The reason can be summarized as vast insensitivity in the interpretations of the characters, including what can charitably be called a very free approach to translation.

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Tue Jun 26, 2007 1:32 am Mr. Tines wrote: The reason can be summarized as vast insensitivity in the interpretations of the characters, including what can charitably be called a very free approach to translation.

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Tue Jun 26, 2007 1:56 am spike spencer?! humph. i don't know who he is either. But it seems as that is only famous for the Shinji role. whatever.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Tue Jun 26, 2007 7:57 am AWL changed a bunch of sound effects in EoE to make it "cooler."

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Tue Jun 26, 2007 8:36 am Don't listen then? I can't watch Akira in Japanese, there's just no way. And I found the english Tenchi Muyo-cast to be preferable too. But when a lot of people agree, outsiders tend to see these things in pretty much black and white. There are pros and cons here too, ya know. For starters; me and some of the people on these boards really liked Allison Keith's Misato. Others didn't.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Tue Jun 26, 2007 9:21 am Mr. Tines wrote: The reason can be summarized as vast insensitivity in the interpretations of the characters, including what can charitably be called a very free approach to translation.

[]:             Quote Post by Morzella » Tue Jun 26, 2007 9:50 am Some of the stuff ADV did for Eva wasn't right. I'm a pureist in all things. Stick to the origanal I say. To take the LOTR comparison, I bitterly hated any change no matter how good the reason. Thus once I knew about AWL sound affect changes I hated them. Once I knew. With a translation I am always more forgiveing. This is not change for changes sake but necceasary so people like me can enjoy something easier. Also a straight translation would be terribul, either not makeing sense or loseing something. For this I will chuck in in a new compparison as LOTR is English anyway. Currently I am reading Dantes Divine Comdey. It's Itlian and there is no way I am learing Itlian to read it as I am seemingly incapabul of learing new languages (I strugle enougth with English). So I am reading a translation. If changes had been made such as "I decided to put these people in hell instead because I decided it would make more sense", I would abe most angry throw the book down in rage and find a diffrent copy. However changes like "The original Itlian used the word Jhfaj here which translates as Morning. However as this part of the poem was an alletraion in the original I have used the word Hello to keep this effect". So mabay the ADV translation doesn't live up to this but translateing is hard work in my oppion so i do forgive them on this. Greenfield talks about Eva like he created it

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Jun 26, 2007 10:30 am Mr. Tines wrote: The reason can be summarized as vast insensitivity in the interpretations of the characters, including what can charitably be called a very free approach to translation.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Tue Jun 26, 2007 11:01 am BrikHaus wrote: Personally, I don't hate any of the ADV crew, but I think they are bunch of idiots. Greenfield talks about Eva like he created it, and AWL changed a bunch of sound effects in EoE to make it "cooler." Sheesh. :roll:

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jun 26, 2007 11:27 am Mr. Tines wrote: The reason can be summarized as vast insensitivity in the interpretations of the characters, including what can charitably be called a very free approach to translation.

[]:             Quote Post by Szmitten » Tue Jun 26, 2007 12:05 pm My beef is from the perspective of an artist.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Tue Jun 26, 2007 12:11 pm Szmitten wrote: I myself generally don't like most English speaking voice actors because they can't really act.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jun 26, 2007 12:40 pm BrikHaus wrote: They're just there to switch on one of many "Mr/Mrs Funny-Voice". I'd much rather have a teenage actress play Asuka than a 30 year old woman, you can just hear it.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Jun 26, 2007 12:43 pm Reichu wrote: But the majority viewpoint is better. Otherwise it wouldn't be the majority.

Why should I "not like" ADV, the English-production cast and crew, Tiffany Grant, Amanda Winn-Lee, etc.
